item.title: “The Wild Party” Returns
item.description: Also: FKA twigs, “Mother Russia,” Caravaggio, and more.

item.title: Martin Parr’s Eye for Human Folly
item.description: The British photographer spent his career examining appetites and the contradictions they engender.
item.dc:creator: ByLauren Collins

item.title: Trump’s Mass-Detention Campaign
item.description: Even with Kristi Noem gone, the Administration’s immigration agenda shows no signs of flagging—in fact, it is leading toward a new humanitarian and legal crisis.
item.dc:creator: ByJonathan Blitzer

item.title: Doctor Mike’s Internet Medicine
item.description: Mikhail Varshavski,People’s“sexiest doctor,” has a medical degree—and millions of fans on YouTube, where he debunks R.F.K., Jr., interviews Kamala Harris, and analyzes “The Pitt.”
item.dc:creator: ByAndrew Trunsky

item.title: Wendy Red Star Gets Her Bag
item.description: The artist, whose new show centers on the history of trade beads, shops for a knockoff Louis Vuitton bag on Canal Street and ruminates on authenticity.
item.dc:creator: ByEmma Allen

item.title: Junior LaBeija, Master of Ceremonies
item.description: As the ballroom legend makes their Broadway début in “Cats: The Jellicle Ball,” they reflect on accessorizing with raw chicken and dressing like Al Capone.
item.dc:creator: ByMichael Schulman

item.title: How to Be Your Own Super
item.description: Doorknob troubles? No sweat—an Upper West Side handyman is helping the helpless, with a beginner’s class on how to fix your (many) apartment problems.
item.dc:creator: ByDiego Lasarte

item.title: What’s Behind Trump’s New World Disorder?
item.description: A foreign policy freed of liberal pretenses and imperial ambitions could lead to restraint—or, as the Iran attack shows, simply license hit-and-run belligerence.
item.dc:creator: ByDaniel Immerwahr

item.title: How Doodles Became the Dog du Jour
item.description: Poodle crossbreeds have grown overwhelmingly popular, sparking controversy in dog parks and kennel clubs alike.
item.dc:creator: ByJohn Seabrook

item.title: Lisa Kudrow Is Back—Again
item.description: Twenty-two years after the end of “Friends,” the actress returns with a new installment of “The Comeback.”
item.dc:creator: ByMichael Schulman

item.title: How Zac Posen Went from Making Ball Gowns to Remaking the Gap
item.description: The fashion designer was brought on as Gap Inc.’s creative director to help restore the company’s cultural relevance. Has the Gapaissance arrived?
item.dc:creator: ByRachel Syme

item.title: Rolling Out Our New A.I. Tools
item.description: Internal memo: Meet our new suite of A.I.-optimized losers and douche bags. Although they are fully agentic, we’re sure they will annoy you in all the ways you’re accustomed to.
item.dc:creator: ByLizzie WiddicombeandPaige Ferrari

item.title: “My Balenciaga”
item.description: It could have been an experiment by the master. An early draft. A failed caprice.
item.dc:creator: ByHan Ong

item.title: How Arsenio Hall Shook Up Late Night
item.description: His show became the epicenter of early-nineties cool, with the decade’s biggest names, from Tom Cruise to Bill Clinton, stopping by to earn street cred.
item.dc:creator: ByJennifer Wilson

item.title: Briefly Noted
item.description: “On Morrison,” “Scale Boy,” “Sisters in Yellow,” and “White River Crossing.”

item.title: Who Bankrolled the American Revolution?
item.description: Our history too often sidesteps the question of finances. But sonorous ideals don’t keep an army supplied with uniforms, guns, and grub.
item.dc:creator: ByAdam Gopnik

item.title: Can Psychoanalysis Help You Get the Life You Want?
item.description: In a new book, Adam Phillips wages a playful war on the strictures of traditional talk therapy.
item.dc:creator: ByKaty Waldman

item.title: What Egon Schiele Saw at the Hospital
item.description: At the Neue Galerie, a show suggests that the artist’s raw, contorted depictions of the body were influenced by a formative relationship with a doctor.
item.dc:creator: ByZachary Fine

item.title: Two Playwrights Tackle Father Figures
item.description: Clare Barron’s “You Got Older” is a rare play about a good dad. Wallace Shawn’s “What We Did Before Our Moth Days” is defiantly tender about an amoral one.
item.dc:creator: ByEmily Nussbaum

item.title: “Project Hail Mary”: In Space, No One Should Hear Your Glib Jokes
item.description: In Phil Lord and Christopher Miller’s adaptation of Andy Weir’s novel, Ryan Gosling’s star power fuels an unlikely tale of far-flung friendship.
item.dc:creator: ByJustin Chang

item.title: “Réservoir”
item.description: “This is the season of crushing elder box leaves with our feet.”
item.dc:creator: ByTova Gannana

item.title: “Then”
item.description: “Then the full / moon rose / & filled the / windows.”
item.dc:creator: ByJorie Graham

item.title: Cartoon Caption Contest
item.description: Submit a caption, plus vote on other entries and finalists.

item.title: Cartoons from the Issue
item.description: Drawings from the March 23, 2026, magazine.
